name: Slugs and snails
summary: Common pests that damage seedlings, leafy greens, brassicas, and young transplants.
actions: Check plants at night or early morning
Remove hiding places such as boards, dense weeds, and old pots
prevention: Avoid leaving trays and pots sitting in wet corners
Keep paths and bed edges tidy
Raise seedlings before planting out if pressure is high
seasonNotes: Most noticeable in damp, mild conditions and after rain.

name: White butterfly caterpillars
summary: Green caterpillars that feed on brassicas such as broccoli, cabbage, kale, and cauliflower.
actions: Remove eggs and caterpillars by hand
Use fine insect netting before butterflies lay eggs
Keep brassicas grouped so they are easier to protect
prevention: Avoid leaving old brassica plants in the garden
seasonNotes: Most active through warmer months when butterflies are present.

name: Aphids
summary: Small sap-sucking insects that cluster on soft shoots, leaf undersides, and new growth.
signs: Clusters of small insects on new growth
actions: Encourage beneficial insects by planting flowers nearby
prevention: Keep plants growing steadily rather than stressed
seasonNotes: Often builds up in spring and warm sheltered conditions.

name: Powdery mildew
summary: Fungal disease that appears as pale powdery patches, especially on cucurbits and stressed plants.
signs: White or grey powdery patches on leaves
Worse on crowded or stressed plants
actions: Keep plants evenly watered during dry spells
seasonNotes: Common later in the season, especially when plants are stressed.

name: Blossom end rot
summary: A watering and calcium uptake problem causing dark sunken patches at the blossom end of fruit.
signs: Dark sunken patch at the base of fruit
Affected fruit often appears while plant otherwise looks healthy
actions: Mulch soil to reduce moisture swings
prevention: Avoid letting containers dry out completely
Avoid root disturbance once fruiting starts
seasonNotes: Often appears during warm weather or when containers dry out.

name: Bolting
summary: Plants switch from leafy growth to flowering, often making leaves bitter or harvest quality poor.
signs: Rapid change after heat or stress
actions: Remove bolted plants if quality is poor
Use partial shade in warm conditions
prevention: Sow cool-season crops at the right time
seasonNotes: Common as temperatures rise or when plants experience stress.
